claims.claim_text: Whisper-family models combine multilingual speech recognition, speech translation, and language identification in one model architecture.
claims.rationale: The official Whisper README describes Whisper as a multitasking model that performs multilingual speech recognition, speech translation, and language identification.

claims.claim_text: The official Whisper model table lists tiny/base/small/medium/large at 39M/74M/244M/769M/1550M parameters with approximate VRAM of ~1/~1/~2/~5/~10 GB.
claims.rationale: The official README includes the model-size table with parameter counts, approximate VRAM, and relative speed for tiny through large.

claims.claim_text: Whisper large-v2 is a 1550M multilingual checkpoint.
claims.rationale: The official Hugging Face model card snippet for openai/whisper-large-v2 lists large-v2 at 1550M parameters.

claims.claim_text: Whisper large-v3 is a 1550M multilingual checkpoint.
claims.rationale: The official Hugging Face model card snippet for openai/whisper-large-v3 lists large-v3 at 1550M parameters.

claims.claim_text: faster-whisper is a CTranslate2-based Whisper implementation and its published benchmarks report lower memory use and up to 4x faster inference under comparable settings.
claims.rationale: The faster-whisper README explicitly says it is a CTranslate2 reimplementation and states up to 4x faster inference with less memory, with benchmark tables on the same page.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI's TTS endpoint provides built-in voices and supports streamed audio output.
claims.span_hint: line 341 and lines 347-380 | section: Text-to-Speech and Voice Design
claims.rationale: OpenAI's text-to-speech docs say the endpoint has built-in voices and can provide realtime audio output using streaming.

claims.claim_text: Amazon Polly is a managed TTS service that can return audio streams and offers custom Brand Voice engagements.
claims.span_hint: line 342 | section: Text-to-Speech and Voice Design
claims.rationale: AWS's Polly documentation overview says the API returns audio streams and describes Brand Voice as a custom neural TTS offering.

claims.claim_text: Google Cloud Text-to-Speech converts text or SSML into natural human speech and documents supported voices and streaming-related usage.
claims.span_hint: line 343 | section: Text-to-Speech and Voice Design
claims.rationale: Google's Cloud Text-to-Speech docs say the service converts text or SSML into natural human speech and link supported voices plus streaming guides.

claims.claim_text: Coqui TTS is an open-source text-to-speech toolkit.
claims.span_hint: line 344 | section: Text-to-Speech and Voice Design
claims.rationale: The project's GitHub repository describes Coqui TTS as an open-source deep learning toolkit for text-to-speech.

claims.claim_text: Bark is an open-source text-prompted generative audio model.
claims.span_hint: line 345 | section: Text-to-Speech and Voice Design
claims.rationale: The Bark repository describes the project as a text-prompted generative audio model.

claims.claim_text: ElevenLabs offers managed TTS with low-latency model options and voice-cloning features.
claims.span_hint: line 340 | section: Text-to-Speech and Voice Design
claims.rationale: This is a real vendor-capability claim, but the relevant official ElevenLabs help/docs host is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes exposes GPU resources such as `nvidia.com/gpu` through device plugins.
claims.span_hint: lines 608-610 and 665-673 | section: Production Deployment on Kubernetes v1.35+
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes GPU scheduling docs explicitly describe stable GPU support via device plugins and schedulable resources such as `nvidia.com/gpu`.

claims.claim_text: Deepgram uses usage-based pricing and emphasizes streaming STT and voice-agent workflows.
claims.span_hint: line 843 | section: Economics, Reliability, and Evaluation
claims.rationale: Deepgram's official pricing and product pages are off-allowlist, but the claim is real and should be cited from the vendor's own site.

claims.claim_text: AssemblyAI uses usage-based pricing and offers streaming speech-to-text features for voice AI workloads.
claims.span_hint: line 844 | section: Economics, Reliability, and Evaluation
claims.rationale: AssemblyAI's official pricing/docs are off-allowlist, but the claim is real and vendor-specific, so allowlist expansion is the honest path.

claims.claim_text: IBM's Shoebox was presented as the world's first speech-recognition system and understood the digits zero through nine plus six command words.
claims.rationale: IBM's history page explicitly calls Shoebox the world's first speech-recognition system and lists ten digits plus six command words.

claims.claim_text: The faster-whisper project publishes benchmarks showing speed and memory improvements over `openai/whisper`, with results depending on hardware and settings.
claims.rationale: The faster-whisper README includes explicit benchmark tables and notes the hardware/setup context for those comparisons.

further_reading.title: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary Whisper paper for training data scale, multilingual behavior, robustness, and architecture details.

further_reading.title: faster-whisper README and Benchmarks
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful for runtime tradeoffs, quantization options, and published benchmark methodology.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes GPU Scheduling
further_reading.why_relevant: Best upstream reference for `nvidia.com/gpu`, device plugins, and GPU scheduling behavior in clusters.

further_reading.title: pyannote Speaker Diarization 3.1
further_reading.why_relevant: Helpful background for the module's multi-speaker section and the practical diarization pipeline used in examples.

notes: No shared citation pool exists for multimodal-ai yet, so supported claims use inline proposed_url with empty source_ids. Code blocks, diagrams, quiz prompts, and hands-on exercise steps were excluded per the task rules.
